feedback.text: I believe that although this new law would greatly improve consumer rights, there are even more problems and injustices about what this should protect us. 1. Ideological censorship imposed on unfair and even illegal forms: A well-known and widely spoken case on the internet in the last week is Steam, a company selling digital video games, which has been forced to remove certain video games due to pressure from payment processors such as VISA and Mastercard, which were previously pressured by an activist group. Although few people take away these games, which were mostly on unpleasant sex topics, they remain an artistic expression and a technically legal product. However, what is even more worried by players from all over the world (including myself) is what these activist groups can try to censor games (or other products) which are of interest to the average player, just because they touch on controversial topics. A great example of this is that the above-mentioned activist group tried to ban the titles "Grand Theft Auto V", the second most sold video game in the world and "Detroit: Become Human ", a game that is also successful, just because it represents (without glorifying) violence against women and children. And although these two games are safe today, it is extremely likely what this activist organisation or others try to do what are censorship or ban future video games despite what these are entirely legal. Another similar case is ‘Ready or Not’ a video game what represents US SWAT teams, their work to keep the streets safe and the psychological impact what operations can have on agents. Logically, this game dealt with very hard and unpleasant topics such as human trafficking, torture or child abuse. This game was initially only available on PC, a platform on which it enjoyed (almost) complete freedom to deal with these issues and represent the heroic work of the police. However, this game has recently been launched in consoles, for which, according to the creators of Ready or Not (Void Interactive, who, if my information is correct, are from the Republic of Ireland) have had to censor the game on the orders of the companies selling the consoles (Sony and Microsoft), removing or replacing the most unpleasant parts of this title, what does the psychological impact of what they have on the player is minor, and the less understandable psychological impact of agents, what was a significant part of history. Not only that, but also some of the content that has been censored are present in other games, such as the break-up of carcasses or nuisance, and still these games can be bought and played in the same consoles today. Those titles include fallout 3, New Vegas, 4 and 76, Cyberpunk 2077, Red Dead Redemption 2 and Grand Theft Auto V. 2. Art destruction: As many players and some governments like Germany consider video games to be art and should therefore be protected in order to be enjoyed by future generations, however, this does not stop publishers and other companies involved in the creation of video games from destroying these forms of art or doing what is more difficult to access, everything completely unnecessary. A clear case of this is "Spec Ops: The Line ", a game that was still relatively recently sold, but now it cannot be bought and no free access to the title has been given. People who already bought this game in the past still have access, but what we have not bought, we cannot access it, regardless of the money we are willing to pay. I do not know whether this title can be accessed from a PC using emulsifiers.
